---
title: "Biostatistics Courses"
pagetitle: "Biostatistics Courses"
toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.kicker}
OPEN CURRICULUM · R · QUARTO · MIT
:::
# Biostatistics Courses

::: {.lead}
A four-course ladder from first principles to modern statistical learning,
built for biomedical researchers who want to stop guessing about statistics.
:::
:::

## What this is

This site hosts a complete, opinionated biostatistics programme written in
R and Quarto. It runs from the scientific process and data hygiene through
regression, study design, causal inference, and modern statistical learning.
Every lab is runnable on a laptop, every concept is introduced by way of a
plot before a formula, and every inference lab follows the same five-step
template so the shape of statistical reasoning becomes familiar long before
the mathematics stops being intimidating. The curriculum is MIT-licensed
and self-contained: no external data files, no institutional login, no
exams. Clone the repository, open an R session, and work through it.

## Who this is for

The primary audience is PhD students, clinicians, postdocs, and research
engineers in the biomedical sciences — anyone who needs to read a methods
section critically, design a defensible study, analyse their own data, and
report the result without sweeping the uncertainty under the rug. A working
knowledge of basic R and high-school algebra is enough to start Course 1.
The later courses assume familiarity with regression but build everything
else from the ground up.

## The four courses

::: {.card-grid}

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Course 1 · Introductory
:::
### [Foundations of Biostatistics with R](course1_foundations/index.qmd)
The scientific process, data hygiene, probability, sampling, and the core
hypothesis tests that anchor everything else. 4 weeks · 20 labs.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Course 2 · Intermediate
:::
### [Regression, ANOVA & Model Diagnostics](course2_regression/index.qmd)
Linear models, ANOVA, GLMs, diagnostics, calibration, and honest model
evaluation. 4 weeks · 20 labs.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Course 3 · Advanced
:::
### [Study Design, Longitudinal Data & Causal Inference](course3_design_causal/index.qmd)
Designing studies; handling missing, clustered, and time-to-event data;
and making causal claims with care. 4 weeks · 20 labs.
:::

::: {.card}
::: {.kicker}
Course 4 · Specialist
:::
### [Modern Statistical Learning & High-Dimensional Biomedicine](course4_ml_highdim/index.qmd)
Regularisation, tree ensembles, Bayesian modelling, omics, and
reproducibility at scale. 4 weeks · 20 labs.
:::

:::

## The five-step template

Every inference lab marches through the same five steps:
**Hypothesis → Visualise → Assumptions → Conduct → Conclude.**
Workflow labs use the variant **Goal → Approach → Execution → Check → Report.**
The sequence is not arbitrary: it mirrors the order in which a Methods
section is written. State what you are asking, look at the data before you
model it, check whether your chosen method is defensible, run it, and then
write down what you found with an effect size and an interval. After
twenty repetitions of this rhythm the scaffolding becomes invisible and
the statistics starts to feel like a thought process rather than a recipe.

## One source, two formats

Every lab in this curriculum is written once and rendered twice. A single
`.qmd` file produces a long-form article for reading and a
[Reveal.js](https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/) slide deck
for teaching. The article carries the prose, the code, and the
discussion; the deck distils the essentials into slides you can present
at a group meeting or a seminar. One `quarto render` builds both.

## Further along

- [Get Started](get_started.qmd) — install, clone, and render the site locally.
- [Schedule](schedule.qmd) — a linked index of every lab, every deck, every script.
- [Cheatsheets](cheatsheets.qmd) — sixteen dense one-pagers, one per week, HTML + PDF.
- [Interactive apps](shiny.qmd) — Shiny companions to the labs.
- [Research workflow](appendices/research_workflow.qmd), [Decision tree](appendices/decision_tree.qmd), [Glossary](appendices/glossary.qmd), [Common errors](appendices/common_errors.qmd), [Writing a report](appendices/writing_a_report.qmd).
- [References](references.qmd) — the sources and teaching ecosystems we drew on.
- [Acknowledgements](acknowledgements.qmd) — the full list of people whose work made this curriculum possible.
